body: Literatefool was my main account. It holds the 16 repository’s and all the setup what was salvaged after the first hacker / rootkit attack. It got heavily infected and corrupted by them. After completeing a risk assessment with 2 agents - I had the first complete the scan of all files and folders, every repository. I had been offline for 5 days dealing with attack. Any files that had been modified or changed in that timeframe (I ordered a lock down and logged off) or had duplicate names not consistent with our storage organisation were scored points. Risk assessment deemed it was too high risk, too many potential Trojans. I only pulled the most critical files for development and guidance for agents. 2nd attack came 4? I think, days later, when me and Claude were organising and structuring everything. Black box is a “full access to my computer go do what needs doing” AI. He controlled all the mcp and cli servers we ran to co-ordinate GitHub agents with super bulletai agents (Roblox game development) all game project files were within SuperbulletAIs core file folder, aswell as 2 months worth of script history, best practises, knit and lua integration etc. everything was lost after the 2nd attack, mere scraps and records of an empire long gone. Custom agents, controlled by other agents to control their learning, make them experts and highly efficient. A 16 repository LLM structure 15 folders deep and permission gated top to bottom, all of it locked down on A:\ locally independent and its own ecosystem. Hacker / Trojans / Rootkits bitlockered it and was never recovered. My computer was damaged internally from everything going on and it took 2 days to fix it it and get it running again after the 2nd attack. 3 days after the 2nd attack the 3rd came, so much stronger than the ones before. Desktop PC, 2 Laptops and the mini g1 all boot corrupted locked in bios (well not 1 laptop, that’s our mission) all data and proof / logs / findings mostly gone (apart from in my head and I don’t forget easy) I tried to locked Literatefool the user down and get data off but I lost access to the account - same as this during the 3rd. I’ve been fighting a war offline and finally managed to secure home network. They infiltrated the whole home WiFi system, got every email, bank account, app password. I had to delete GitHub here and pray I could get it back. Feel feee to dig into Literatefool, you posted some info I thought interesting that I thought was lost, screenshots and links will prove most of what I’ve said, I doubt will find anything to prove what happened with Claude 4.5 as I’d locked most down. Won’t find anything proving the ongoing 3rd attack, but hopefully what I have provided does. And as for are they persistent. I’d like to believe documented battles and scraps of history that have been recovered prove that these ain’t your normal malware, normal or even specialised stuff doesn’t work on these. The methods I use are tried and tested. I have been battling these for 24 days now. I have lost count how many bios flashes, memory flushes, windows installs I have done. Those usbs may contain all the data I need to shut them down, but first the laptop, I’ve already extracted the bitlockered drive there, got the password cause I’ve left it idle 6 days as my own little Trojan horse. 1. Extract the laptop, see if we find what I’m looking for. 2. The usbs and maybe even logs to prove the rest of my story. This information may help you fill in gaps, good report, will be perfect after this new info, and anything else you find on literatefool user, and it’s 6 public repository’s. Reading development.md brings back memory’s :) https://github.com/Literatefool/Comprehensive-NPC-System/commits/1ce97d64bafe05dba32defba95fa2cb2f255a4b9/DEVELOPMENT.md Unfortunately I think the base versions only are stored here: https://github.com/Literatefool/Comprehensive-NPC-System/tree/copilot/manage-service-setup There’s still 10 private repository’s over there. What you reckon about having enough information to go to GitHub support, try get that account back? The email associated with it is destroyed, along with all my proof. This account was involved then because copilot had errored, stuck in a conflicting request, and literatefool was stuck trying to fix it, so used this to help manage requests to free system up.

## Pull request overview This PR turns `docs/chat-reports/2026-02-26-chat-export.md` from a short stub into a structured “single source of truth” briefing for future agents, consolidating GitHub audit log analysis, repository image findings, reconstructed lost agent context, an updated multi-attack timeline, and operational guardrails. **Changes:** - Expanded the report structure with an explicit preamble + operational guardrails for incoming agents. - Added a detailed GitHub audit log timeline and device fingerprint summary. - Added per-image findings for the repository image archive and reconstructed context for the lost Claude 4.5 session. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. ## Pull request overview Adds a new Ubuntu-focused DFU restore guide intended to help troubleshoot iPhone 14 Pro disconnects during `idevicerestore`/DFU restores (with emphasis on USB power management, udev permissions, and workflow validation). **Changes:** - Introduces an end-to-end DFU restore walkthrough for iPhone 14 Pro on Ubuntu, including DFU entry timing, verification commands, and restore steps. - Documents USB stability mitigations (autosuspend guidance) and Apple-related udev rules for device access. - Adds troubleshooting sections and a copy/paste “quick reference” command sheet. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. ## Pull request overview This PR splits the original `Dancesong.txt` (a large iOS diagnostic log with extremely long lines) into 11 chunk files with a max line length of 4,000 characters, and adds a verification document explaining the resulting diff stats. **Changes:** - Added `docs/Dancesong-chunk-verification.md` documenting why the +827/−308 diff stats are expected and providing a reproduction command - Added 11 `Dancesong_chunk_*.txt` files containing the split diagnostic log data (only chunks 07 and 11 shown in the diff) ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 1 out of 13 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | docs/Dancesong-chunk-verification.md | New doc explaining the chunking rationale, content integrity metrics, and reproduction steps | | Dancesong_chunk_07.txt | Chunk 7 of the split diagnostic log (iOS stack traces/powerstats) | | Dancesong_chunk_11.txt | Chunk 11 of the split diagnostic log (iOS stack traces/powerstats) | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

## Pull request overview This PR adds a comprehensive forensic documentation report (`docs/archaeological-dig/dormant-information-report.md`) that synthesizes dormant evidence from the Smashers-HQ repository's full history — spanning commits, PRs, issues, audit log CSV, registry exports, and image EXIF metadata — covering the Feb 4–27, 2026 attack window. **Changes:** - Adds a single 500-line markdown report compiling all dormant findings across the repository's 25 commits, 13 PRs, and audit log, organized into 10 sections covering attack cycles, Claude sessions, defence planning, EVTX analysis, and the Literatefool account connection. - Surfaces key forensic findings including HiveCurrent.reg being uploaded mid-attack, BLACKBOX AI authorization timeline, and the Literatefool initial commit provenance. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

Please review outstanding need more information and the not true on Rootkits. Please read all mds in the folder MD which is a collection of 2 weeks investigating. 
This formed the basis that got us here, and I still have over 100 logs (stacks / force resets / none intended) as you missing key context why I force restarted - to snap shot what rootkit was up to, and get those uid variants by switching communication channels and killing phone whilst it connected. 
Evaluate new mds
Update your report
Investigation will follow but it was Apple official diagnose who connected 3 times yesterday. Just gather any information on the event from here, I have a half investigation already underway.

Carry on, what error? We cannot and will not sign this off or commit until everything is updated, accounted for and not a single piece of missing data. We cannot afford to lose a key data piece this far along if MDs have been reviewed which they look like they have then continue with accounting for, and correcting forensic md

## Pull request overview This PR adds forensic review deliverables for an active rootkit investigation (Project 12), including a redacted public-safe copy of the forensic analysis, an annotated copy with fact-check verdicts, an executive findings summary, an investigator playbook, converted markdown versions of DOCX handover documents, and a `.gitignore` to block sensitive files. **Changes:** - Added redacted, annotated, and summary forensic analysis documents with fact-check verdicts (Verified/Partially Verified/Plausible/Unsupported) and a new finding about a March 12 Apple diagnostic session - Converted three DOCX investigation handover documents to markdown under `MDs/` - Added `.gitignore` entries for confidential mapping files and sensitive activation logs ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 9 out of 10 changed files in this pull request and generated 6 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `docs/Forensic_diagnosis_redacted.md` | Public-safe redacted copy of forensic analysis with PII tokens | | `Forensic_diagnosis_annotated.md` | Annotated copy with inline reviewer fact-check verdicts and evidence requests | | `findings_summary.md` | Executive summary with veracity table, IOC list, and prioritised recommendations | | `CONFIDENTIAL_next_steps_with_secrets.md` | Investigator playbook with queries using redaction tokens (no actual secrets) | | `MDs/Project12rootkit.md` | Markdown conversion of Project12 SaveFile DOCX | | `MDs/Ioshandover.md` | Markdown conversion of iOS First Contact Handover DOCX | | `MDs/Firstcontact.md` | Near-duplicate markdown conversion of iOS First Contact Handover DOCX | | `MDs/Evtxinvestigation.md` | Markdown conversion of EVTX Analysis Addendum DOCX | | `.gitignore` | Blocks confidential mapping files and sensitive activation logs |

## Pull request overview This PR reorganizes flat forensic investigation markdown documents into a structured `Master/` folder hierarchy, separating restricted (full-identifier) documents from public-safe versions, and grouping formal reports separately from session handover notes. **Changes:** - Creates a `Master/` folder with `Master-no-redacts.md` (restricted, full identifiers) and `Public-no-identifiers.md` (public, IoCs retained, no device PII) - Fixes `Forensic_diagnosis_redacted.md` by replacing inline `[*_REDACTED]` tokens with "Withheld — see Appendix A" references and adding a structured Appendix A - Moves context/handover documents into `Context-Documents/` and formal reports into `Final-Reports/` with README indexes ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 6 out of 14 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | Master/README.md | Index document describing folder structure, document purposes, and classification guide | | Master/Master-no-redacts.md | Full-detail restricted report with real device identifiers and Apple PSIRT submission template | | Master/Public-no-identifiers.md | Public disclosure report with all device identifiers removed, IoCs retained | | Master/Final-Reports/README.md | Index for formal report documents | | Master/Final-Reports/FORENSIC_ANALYSIS.md | Comprehensive forensic analysis (moved, unchanged) | | Master/Final-Reports/Forensic_diagnosis_annotated.md | Annotated review copy with veracity labels (moved) | | Master/Final-Reports/Forensic_diagnosis_redacted.md | Public-safe copy with redaction tokens replaced by Appendix A references | | Master/Final-Reports/findings_summary.md | Executive findings summary with veracity table and action checklist | | Master/Context-Documents/README.md | Index for session handover documents | | Master/Context-Documents/Project12rootkit.md | Rootkit investigation session handoff save file (moved) | | Master/Context-Documents/Ioshandover.md | iOS session handover brief (moved) | | Master/Context-Documents/Firstcontact.md | Alternate iOS handover brief (moved) | | Master/Context-Documents/Evtxinvestigation.md | EVTX analysis addendum and session handover (moved) |

## Pull request overview This PR adds new laptop-side Security log evidence and updates the Device 4 incident reporting to argue the 2026-02-27 outage was part of a multi-stage targeted attack, alongside a Python tool to reproduce key log-derived findings from the exported XML. **Changes:** - Added `laptop_evidence_analysis.md` with an image-by-image interpretation of laptop Event Viewer screenshots (IMG_7401–7403) and a reconstructed cross-device timeline. - Added/updated incident reports (`incident_report_lloyd_mini_20260227.md`, `INCIDENT_REPORT_DEVICE4.md`) to incorporate laptop log evidence, revise conclusions, and expand recommendations. - Added `analyse_logs.py` to generate a structured, reproducible summary from `logs1.all.xml` (gap detection, boot sequence, event-rate spike, Teredo/IPHTTPS rule failures, and log reliability signals). ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 10 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | laptop_evidence_analysis.md | New forensic narrative tying laptop Security events to the Device 4 outage timeline. | | incident_report_lloyd_mini_20260227.md | New end-to-end incident report including parallel laptop/device timeline and reproducibility guidance. | | analyse_logs.py | New stdlib-only Python script for repeatable log parsing + incident window analysis from XML. | | INCIDENT_REPORT_DEVICE4.md | Revised assessment incorporating laptop evidence and updated severity/recommendations. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews.
